codeReview.summary: The merge largely completed the module consolidation and import rewrites, but `toArchiveTimestamp` no longer preserves prior behavior for ISO strings without milliseconds, which violates the feature's behavior-preservation requirement.

issues.description: Regression in `toArchiveTimestamp`: `value.replace(/[-:]/g, "").replace(/\.(\d{3})Z$/, ".$1")` leaves a trailing `Z` for inputs without fractional seconds (e.g. `2025-04-05T19:42:13Z` -> `20250405T194213Z`). The previous implementation in `src/runtime/time.ts` removed `Z` unconditionally (`replace("Z", "")`). This breaks the 'preserve exact behavior' expectation for the exported utility.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The core-worker skill requires writing failing tests first for observable behavior, but this refactor feature appears to have relied on existing tests and import rewrites only while still reporting `followedProcedure: true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session `15f53eb7-7513-4925-9060-2403a6cdd68f` shows no new red-first test flow before edits; handoff marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` true and `tests.added` is empty.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation assertion wording says time utilities preserve exact behavior, but the practical evidence target/tests cover only one millisecond-formatted input, leaving compatibility cases unguarded.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md VAL-TRANS-005 (lines 465-468) and tests/archive-collision.test.ts only assert `toArchiveTimestamp('2025-04-05T19:42:13.482Z')` shape; the regression at src/runtime/util.ts:6 for non-millisecond ISO strings is not exercised.

summary: Reviewed feature `m4-constants-util-merge` (commit `080edf8`), including handoff, transcript skeleton, and diff. Consolidation was completed, but a blocking behavior regression exists in `toArchiveTimestamp` for non-millisecond ISO input, so the feature review fails.
